title: Cinderella
full_description: Christopher Wheeldon's sparkling, cinematic adaptation created specifically for Dutch National Ballet in 2012. This staging is darker and more poetic than the Cinderella most people are familiar with, based on the original version popularised by the Brothers Grimm, as well as the 1945 Prokofiev ballet. Wheeldon gives more depth and background to the main characters. At the heart of everything is American puppeteer Basil Twist's magical tree, brought forth by the tears the child Cinderella weeps at her mother's grave — from it her carriage appears and in its shade she is married.
ai_summary: Christopher Wheeldon's Cinderella for Dutch National Ballet takes a darker, more poetic approach to the fairy tale, grounded in the Brothers Grimm rather than Disney. The centrepiece is Basil Twist's extraordinary living tree — a symbol of Cinderella's spirit, awakened by her grief.
